title: Sabzi Polo ba Mahi (Herb Rice with Fish)
description: The traditional dish for the Persian New Year (Nowruz), featuring fluffy rice layered with abundant fresh herbs, served alongside pan-fried or baked fish.

ingredients.note: Washed and soaked for 2 hours in salted water
Like tilapia, cod, or traditional whitefish
Ground, bloomed in 2 tbsp hot water

instructions.text: Combine all the chopped fresh herbs (parsley, cilantro, dill, chives) in a large bowl and mix well.

Bring 8 cups of water with 2 tbsp salt to a boil in a large pot. Drain the soaked rice and add it to the boiling water. Boil for 5-7 minutes until the rice is parboiled (soft on the outside, firm on the inside). Drain in a colander.
In a non-stick pot, heat 2 tbsp of oil. Place a layer of parboiled rice at the bottom, followed by a layer of the mixed herbs. Repeat layering, ending with rice, and shape into a pyramid.

Poke 4-5 holes in the rice pyramid with the handle of a wooden spoon. Pour the melted butter and 2 tbsp of water over the top. Cover the lid with a kitchen towel, seal tightly, and steam on low heat for 45-50 minutes.
While the rice is steaming, prepare the fish. Mix the flour, turmeric, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper on a shallow plate.
Pat the fish fillets dry and dredge them in the seasoned flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the fish fillets for 3-5 minutes per side until golden brown, crispy, and cooked through.
Once the rice is done, gently mix it to distribute the herbs evenly. Mix 1 cup of the cooked herb rice with the bloomed saffron for a colorful garnish.
Serve the Sabzi Polo topped with the saffron-herb rice, accompanied by the fried fish and fresh lemon wedges.

tips: The key to good Sabzi Polo is ensuring the herbs are completely dry before chopping them, otherwise, the rice will become mushy. Traditional Nowruz fish is often smoked whitefish (Mahi Doodi), but fresh fried fish is very popular.
storage_notes: Store rice and fish separately. The rice keeps well for up to 3 days in the fridge. The fish is best eaten fresh but can be refrigerated for 2 days.
serve_with: Kuku Sabzi (herb frittata), pickled garlic (Seer Torshi), and fresh Seville orange halves if in season.

substitutions.note: If fresh aren't available, use 1/3 of the amount in dried Persian herb mixes (Sabzi Polo mix), soaked in water before use.
Salmon is a very common and delicious alternative. It can be pan-fried or baked.
